When a settings change causes data loss across pages, model settings as ownership boundaries rather than one shared payload. Problem pattern: [Bug]: tavily settings gets reset when you go from advanced to basic

lesson:
When an endpoint exposes enumeration or unauthenticated access, remove or restrict the broad route while preserving the minimal legitimate access path. Problem pattern: Remove shared conversations search endpoint

Related https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/pull/13888

It is not necessary for a user to be able to view all shared conversations. Accepted fix pattern: Removes the `/search` and `/count` route handlers from `shared_conversation_router.py`, leaving only the per-ID batch lookup the public viewer needs.; Drops the now-dead service methods (`search_shared_conversation_info`, `count_shared_conversation_info`, the `_apply_filters` helper, and the long-stale `_public_select` Evidence files: enterprise/server/sharing/shared_event_router.py, enterprise/tests/unit/test_sharing/test_shared_event_filtering.py, enterprise/server/sharing/shared_conversation_info_service.py, enterprise/server/sharing/shared_conversation_models.py, enterprise/server/sharing/shared_conversation_router.py, enterprise/server/sharing/sql_shared_conversation_info_service.py. Validation pattern: Verified against a deployed build of this branch on a test instance using a known shared conversation id `d28670c95c224d9481d55f165fb49192`. Review signal: 🟢 **Good taste** - Clean security fix that eliminates unauthenticated enumeration of shared conversations.

lesson:
When adding a new frontend event type, trace the full rendering pipeline instead of adding an isolated component. Problem pattern: Render ACPToolCallEvent in the conversation viewer

When an ACP agent (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI via the Agent Client Protocol) runs tools during a conversation, the agent-server emits `ACPToolCallEvent`s to the event stream. Accepted fix pattern: ACP agents (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI via the Agent Client Protocol) emit `ACPToolCallEvent`s for every tool call they execute. The frontend had **zero renderers** for them, so every tool call was silently dropped and users saw only the final `MessageEvent`.; ACP tool call cards now render through the **same pipeline as regular OpenHands observations** — `GenericEventMessageWrapper` → `getEventContent` → `SuccessIndicator`. No bespoke component, no custom pills, no custom collapse UI. The card shape, status icon, markdown body and truncation behavior all match the rest of the conversation view.; The "kind" differentiator is a translated **`ACP · `** prefix in the title row (e.g. `ACP · Running <cmd>gh pr diff 490</cmd>`) via new `ACTION_MESSAGE$ACP_RUN` / `$ACP_EDIT` / `$ACP_READ` / `$ACP_FETCH` / `$ACP_TOOL` keys that mirror the existing `ACTION_MESSAGE$RUN` pattern with `<cmd>` / `<path>` component wrapping.; Dedup by `tool_call_id` in the `uiEvents` layer so that, once OpenHands/software-agent-sdk#2866 starts streaming `in_progress → completed` updates, the same card updates in place rather than duplicating. Evidence files: poetry.lock, frontend/__tests__/components/v1/chat/event-message-components/acp-tool-call-event-message.test.tsx, frontend/__tests__/utils/handle-event-for-ui.test.ts, frontend/src/components/v1/chat/event-content-helpers/should-render-event.ts, frontend/src/components/v1/chat/event-message-components/acp-tool-call-event-message.tsx, frontend/src/components/v1/chat/event-message-components/index.ts. Validation pattern: - [x] `npm run typecheck` — clean.; - [x] `npx vitest run` — **1957 passing** (+7 over main), 0 regressions.; - [x] `npx eslint` — clean on all new / modified files. Review signal: 🟢 **Good taste** - Clean implementation that elegantly solves the problem of rendering ACP tool calls.

evidence.fix_summary: ACP agents (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI via the Agent Client Protocol) emit `ACPToolCallEvent`s for every tool call they execute. The frontend had **zero renderers** for them, so every tool call was silently dropped and users saw only the final `MessageEvent`.; ACP tool call cards now render through the **same pipeline as regular OpenHands observations** — `GenericEventMessageWrapper` → `getEventContent` → `SuccessIndicator`. No bespoke component, no custom pills, no custom collapse UI. The card shape, status icon, markdown body and truncation behavior all match the rest of the conversation view.; The "kind" differentiator is a translated **`ACP · `** prefix in the title row (e.g. `ACP · Running <cmd>gh pr diff 490</cmd>`) via new `ACTION_MESSAGE$ACP_RUN` / `$ACP_EDIT` / `$ACP_READ` / `$ACP_FETCH` / `$ACP_TOOL` keys that mirror the existing `ACTION_MESSAGE$RUN` pattern with `<cmd>` / `<path>` component wrapping.; Dedup by `tool_call_id` in the `uiEvents` layer so that, once OpenHands/software-agent-sdk#2866 starts streaming `in_progress → completed` updates, the same card updates in place rather than duplicating.
feedback_signals: 🟢 **Good taste** - Clean implementation that elegantly solves the problem of rendering ACP tool calls.

The OpenHands GUI exposes ``llm.api_key`` and ``llm.base_url`` on the LLM settings page, and those are used correctly for the OpenHands (LLM) agent. Accepted fix pattern: Extend `LiveStatusAppConversationService._acp_provider_env` to also emit `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` / `OPENAI_BASE_URL` / `GEMINI_BASE_URL` when `llm.base_url` is set.; Gate `*_BASE_URL` emission on `api_key` also being present, preserving PR #14004's security guarantee: "a provider-default `base_url` alone never clobbers an explicit `OH_AGENT_SERVER_ENV` passthrough".; Mirror the existing SDK forward-compat pattern: prefer `acp_settings.base_url_env_var` when the SDK exposes it (per software-agent-sdk PR #2984 TODO), fall back to a per-server map for the current pinned SDK.; Blank / whitespace-only `base_url` is treated as unset. lesson:
When a settings change causes data loss across pages, model settings as ownership boundaries rather than one shared payload. Problem pattern: Implement Bitbucket Data Center webhook handler for @OpenHands resolver

The `@OpenHands` resolver does not trigger when commenting on Bitbucket Data Center pull requests. Accepted fix pattern: APP-1389: add bitbucket data center resolver for pr comment triggers Evidence files: enterprise/integrations/bitbucket_data_center/bitbucket_dc_manager.py, enterprise/integrations/bitbucket_data_center/bitbucket_dc_v1_callback_processor.py, enterprise/integrations/bitbucket_data_center/bitbucket_dc_view.py, enterprise/integrations/models.py, enterprise/migrations/versions/112_create_bitbucket_dc_webhook_table.py, enterprise/saas_server.py. Validation pattern: Required. Share the steps for the reviewer to be able to test your PR. e.g. You can test by running `npm install` then `npm build dev`.; If you could not test this, say why.; - `enterprise/server/routes/integration/bitbucket_dc.py` — `POST /integration/bitbucket-dc/events`. HMAC-SHA256 verification against the per-repo secret (looked up from `bitbucket_dc_webhook`). Repo identity read from `payload.pullRequest.toRef.repository.{project.key, slug}`. Honors `diagnostics:ping` (DC's webhook test event) with a 200 short-circuit. Dedup key `bbdc:{event_key}:{pr_id}:{comment_id}:{request_id}` with a body-hash fallback. Review signal: **Taste Rating:** 🟡 **Acceptable** - The core integration pattern is sound and mirrors the existing Cloud resolver, but there are important security trade-offs and design workarounds that need addressing.

When using an ACP agent, each tool call in the chat UI is prefixed with `ACP · ` (e.g. Accepted fix pattern: Removes `ACP · ` prefix from all 5 ACP tool call translation keys (75 strings across 15 languages) in `translation.json` so ACP tool call cards render identically to standard OpenHands action cards; Also suppresses `ACPToolCallEvent` cards while `status === "in_progress"` to avoid an empty-content flash before args are populated, with corresponding tests Evidence files: frontend/__tests__/components/v1/chat/event-content-helpers/should-render-event.test.ts, frontend/src/components/v1/chat/event-content-helpers/get-acp-tool-call-content.ts, frontend/src/components/v1/chat/event-content-helpers/get-event-content.tsx, frontend/src/components/v1/chat/event-content-helpers/should-render-event.ts. lesson:
When a settings change causes data loss across pages, model settings as ownership boundaries rather than one shared payload. Problem pattern: ACP tool calls flash empty state before populating with real values

When an ACP agent runs, tool call messages appear in the chat UI immediately with empty arguments (`{}`) and then get updated with the real values a moment later. Accepted fix pattern: ACP emits an `in_progress` event with empty `raw_input: {}` before the `completed` event arrives with real args; The UI was rendering the `in_progress` card immediately, causing a visible `{}` flash, then replacing it when `completed` arrived; Filter out `in_progress` events in `shouldRenderEvent` so the card only appears once, fully populated; `handleEventForUI` already tracks `in_progress` events for dedup (so the `completed` event replaces them in place) — this change simply keeps them off-screen until the terminal state arrives Evidence files: frontend/__tests__/components/v1/chat/event-content-helpers/should-render-event.test.ts, frontend/src/components/v1/chat/event-content-helpers/should-render-event.ts, frontend/__tests__/components/features/chat/switch-profile-button.test.tsx, frontend/__tests__/components/features/conversation/conversation-name.test.tsx, frontend/__tests__/components/features/home/recent-conversation.test.tsx, frontend/__tests__/components/features/user/user-context-menu.test.tsx. Review signal: Fixed by https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/pull/14401

evidence.fix_summary: ACP emits an `in_progress` event with empty `raw_input: {}` before the `completed` event arrives with real args; The UI was rendering the `in_progress` card immediately, causing a visible `{}` flash, then replacing it when `completed` arrived; Filter out `in_progress` events in `shouldRenderEvent` so the card only appears once, fully populated; `handleEventForUI` already tracks `in_progress` events for dedup (so the `completed` event replaces them in place) — this change simply keeps them off-screen until the terminal state arrives
feedback_signals: Fixed by https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/pull/14401

lesson:
For agent-runtime changes, trace lifecycle events, state transitions, tool calls, and error propagation end-to-end. Problem pattern: GCS storage.Client() created per-request causes connection pool exhaustion under load

The `GoogleCloudEventServiceInjector.inject()` and `GoogleCloudSharedEventServiceInjector.inject()` methods create a **new `google.cloud.storage.Client()` on every HTTP request**. Accepted fix pattern: Add a process-wide shared GCS client via `@lru_cache(maxsize=1)` in `google_cloud_event_service.py`; Replace per-request `storage.Client()` calls in both `GoogleCloudEventServiceInjector.inject()` and `GoogleCloudSharedEventServiceInjector.inject()` with the shared client; Remove unused `storage` and `Client` imports from `google_cloud_shared_event_service.py` Evidence files: enterprise/server/sharing/google_cloud_shared_event_service.py, enterprise/tests/unit/test_sharing/test_sharing_shared_event_service.py, openhands/app_server/event/google_cloud_event_service.py. Review signal: 🟢 **Good taste** - Clean, pragmatic fix for a real production issue.

evidence.fix_summary: Add a process-wide shared GCS client via `@lru_cache(maxsize=1)` in `google_cloud_event_service.py`; Replace per-request `storage.Client()` calls in both `GoogleCloudEventServiceInjector.inject()` and `GoogleCloudSharedEventServiceInjector.inject()` with the shared client; Remove unused `storage` and `Client` imports from `google_cloud_shared_event_service.py`
feedback_signals: 🟢 **Good taste** - Clean, pragmatic fix for a real production issue.

lesson:
When a settings change causes data loss across pages, model settings as ownership boundaries rather than one shared payload. Problem pattern: ACP settings: preserve LLM/condenser/MCP config across OpenHands ↔ ACP toggles

## Context

PR #14227 ships a minimum-viable agent-kind switch for the new ACP settings UI: toggling **Settings → Agent** between **OpenHands** and **ACP** no longer 500s, but the new kind always star... Accepted fix pattern: Toggling **Settings → Agent** between OpenHands and ACP no longer loses the outgoing config; `Settings.update()` now snapshots each kind's full config into a new `saved_agent_configs` field and restores it on switch-back; Inline overrides in the same payload as a kind switch apply on top of the restored snapshot; SaaS: adds an `EncryptedJSON` column `saved_agent_configs` to the `User` table (migration 113); pre-migration `NULL` rows fall back to the empty-dict default with no behaviour change Evidence files: tests/integration/README.md, dev_config/python/.pre-commit-config.yaml, enterprise/migrations/versions/113_add_display_name_to_conversation_metadata.py, enterprise/poetry.lock, enterprise/tests/unit/storage/test_saas_sql_app_conversation_info_service.py, frontend/__tests__/components/features/user/user-context-menu.test.tsx. Validation pattern: | `tests/unit/app_server/test_settings_agent_kind_switch.py` | 9 new tests; removed the now-wrong "resets to defaults" test |; | `enterprise/tests/unit/test_saas_settings_store.py` | SaaS round-trip test for `saved_agent_configs` | Review signal: 🟡 **Acceptable** - Solid implementation that solves a real user problem.

evidence.fix_summary: Toggling **Settings → Agent** between OpenHands and ACP no longer loses the outgoing config; `Settings.update()` now snapshots each kind's full config into a new `saved_agent_configs` field and restores it on switch-back; Inline overrides in the same payload as a kind switch apply on top of the restored snapshot; SaaS: adds an `EncryptedJSON` column `saved_agent_configs` to the `User` table (migration 113); pre-migration `NULL` rows fall back to the empty-dict default with no behaviour change
feedback_signals: 🟡 **Acceptable** - Solid implementation that solves a real user problem.

lesson:
For agent-runtime changes, trace lifecycle events, state transitions, tool calls, and error propagation end-to-end. Problem pattern: Sub-agent delegation task tool reports no registered sub-agents

Sub-agent delegation appears to be enabled at the settings/API level on a Replicated/OHE install, and the main agent can see the `task` tool, but actual delegation does not appear to happen. Accepted fix pattern: Add a Settings > Agent page with an Enable sub-agents switch backed by `agent_settings_diff`.; Register built-in agents whenever OpenHands default tools are prepared, and forward sub-agent definitions only when `enable_sub_agents` is enabled.; Use a valid process sandbox working directory and a short tmux socket temp directory for local agent-server startup.; Add schema, conversation-service, settings-form, and process sandbox spec coverage. Evidence files: openhands/app_server/app_conversation/live_status_app_conversation_service.py, tests/unit/app_server/test_live_status_app_conversation_service.py, frontend/__tests__/components/shared/modals/settings/settings-form.test.tsx, frontend/__tests__/routes/manage-org.test.tsx, frontend/src/constants/settings-nav.tsx, frontend/src/hooks/organizations/use-git-conversation-routing.ts. Validation pattern: - `poetry run pytest tests/unit/app_server/test_live_status_app_conversation_service.py -k 'enable_sub_agents'`; - `poetry run pytest tests/unit/app_server/test_settings_api.py -k 'agent_settings_schema'`; - `poetry run pytest tests/unit/app_server/test_agent_server_env_override.py -k 'process_specs_empty_agent_server_env'` Review signal: 🟢 **Good taste** - Clean, pragmatic solution that fixes real issues.

evidence.fix_summary: Add a Settings > Agent page with an Enable sub-agents switch backed by `agent_settings_diff`.; Register built-in agents whenever OpenHands default tools are prepared, and forward sub-agent definitions only when `enable_sub_agents` is enabled.; Use a valid process sandbox working directory and a short tmux socket temp directory for local agent-server startup.; Add schema, conversation-service, settings-form, and process sandbox spec coverage.
feedback_signals: 🟢 **Good taste** - Clean, pragmatic solution that fixes real issues.

lesson:
For test or CI failures, identify the smallest failing path and add focused regression coverage. Problem pattern: [Bug]: OpenRouter configuration still appears to consume OpenHands credits

After configuring OpenRouter as the LLM provider, requests still appear to be routed through OpenHands credits/inference instead of directly through my OpenRouter account. Accepted fix pattern: tighten the frontend budget/credit error classifier so provider-side credit messages are not rewritten as OpenHands billing failures; keep true OpenHands budget and credit-limit messages mapped to the friendly billing banner; add regression coverage for OpenRouter/provider credit text Evidence files: frontend/__tests__/utils/error-handler.test.ts, frontend/src/utils/error-handler.ts. Validation pattern: - `npx -y -p node@22 -p npm@10.5.0 npm run test -- __tests__/utils/error-handler.test.ts`; - `npx -y -p node@22 -p npm@10.5.0 npm run lint:fix -- --quiet` (passes with existing unrelated warnings); - `npx -y -p node@22 -p npm@10.5.0 npm run build` Review signal: This will fixed my issue https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/issues/14419

lesson:
For agent-runtime changes, trace lifecycle events, state transitions, tool calls, and error propagation end-to-end. Problem pattern: Slack resolver: missing critic API key causes generic "unexpected error" instead of user-friendly message

When a user has configured a critic agent in their settings but has not provided the critic's LLM API key, starting a Slack conversation fails with the generic "Uh oh! Accepted fix pattern: Missing critic LLM API key caused a generic Slack error instead of a clear settings message.; Map critic API key validation failures to StartingConvoException; Reuse existing start_job() handler so users get an actionable message; Add unit test for this error path Evidence files: enterprise/integrations/slack/slack_view.py, enterprise/tests/unit/integrations/slack/test_slack_view.py, enterprise/integrations/slack/slack_manager.py, enterprise/tests/unit/test_slack_integration.py. Validation pattern: - Add unit test for this error path Review signal: @openhands Investigate and understand this error.

lesson:
For agent-runtime changes, trace lifecycle events, state transitions, tool calls, and error propagation end-to-end. Problem pattern: Remove null wildcard matching from event callback execution

Remove null wildcard matching from event callback execution lookups and stop constructing callbacks with null routing fields. Accepted fix pattern: Remove `event_kind IS NULL` and `conversation_id IS NULL` wildcard matching from `SQLEventCallbackService.execute_callbacks()`.; Tighten `CreateEventCallbackRequest` / `EventCallback` so new callbacks require concrete `conversation_id` and non-null `event_kind`.; Add `EventCallbackProcessor.get_event_kind()` and use it when registering startup processors.; Register enterprise V1 integration processors for `ConversationStateUpdateEvent` and title callbacks for `MessageEvent`. Evidence files: enterprise/integrations/bitbucket/bitbucket_v1_callback_processor.py, enterprise/integrations/bitbucket_data_center/bitbucket_dc_v1_callback_processor.py, enterprise/integrations/github/github_v1_callback_processor.py, enterprise/integrations/gitlab/gitlab_v1_callback_processor.py, enterprise/integrations/jira/jira_v1_callback_processor.py, enterprise/integrations/jira_dc/jira_dc_v1_callback_processor.py. Validation pattern: - `OPENHANDS_SUPPRESS_BANNER=1 uv run python -m pytest tests/unit/app_server/test_sql_event_callback_service.py tests/unit/app_server/test_webhook_router_auto_title.py tests/unit/app_server/test_set_title_callback_processor.py -q` Review signal: 🟡 **Acceptable** with critical enterprise compatibility issue

**[CRITICAL ISSUES]**
Breaking change for enterprise callback processors - see inline comments.

evidence.fix_summary: Remove `event_kind IS NULL` and `conversation_id IS NULL` wildcard matching from `SQLEventCallbackService.execute_callbacks()`.; Tighten `CreateEventCallbackRequest` / `EventCallback` so new callbacks require concrete `conversation_id` and non-null `event_kind`.; Add `EventCallbackProcessor.get_event_kind()` and use it when registering startup processors.; Register enterprise V1 integration processors for `ConversationStateUpdateEvent` and title callbacks for `MessageEvent`.
feedback_signals:
🟡 **Acceptable** with critical enterprise compatibility issue
